entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud: Newly Created Members Delay In S...
entry.content:
Hello - I have used the create member followed by a data action several times and have not encountered a delay. The data action is able to pick up the new member straight away within the same script without delay timers etc.

When the data action fails I've found instead it's caused by the syntax of the dimension member you are passing across. For example passing a member without a hierarchy you can just pass the ID, but if passing a member to the data action from script that is in a hierarchy, then you have to add the full technical hierarchy information around the ID like below:

var id = InputField_ID.getValue();

var id2 = "[DIMENSION].[H1].&["+id+"]"; // restate ID so that the Data Action can recognise this

..then id2 is passed to the DA

DataAction_1.setParameterValue("DIMENSION",id2);

Just want to check this is not the issue first as I've not experienced any delays before upon master data creation.

David

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Jayshree,

Relating to your ask about MRP integration and the document type needed for the Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer (5HP) here the standard view is as follows.

The process of using MRP to generate Purchase Requisitions can be used also in conjunction with 5HP and specifically herein the special document type needed for the Stock Transfer Order (standard delivered one being NBIC).

In General it bears worth noting, that the Purchase Requisition Item generated from MRP for stock transfer cases will always be categorized to be of type "Stock Transfer" (named as "U" usually, internally referenced as item category 7). 

Thus, regardless of the new scenario 5HP, there is a difference between the Purchase Requisition Item Category and the Purchase Order Item Category in case of intercompany stock transfers.

So the topic is not at all new in any sense. 

What needs to be done is to tell the system, that there needs to be considered a conversion of item category during the PR-PO conversion (again, true for both "existing" intercompany stock transfer as well as for the new advanced intercompany stock transfer). 

This can be achieved via customizing of the Purchase Order Document Type NBIC. To be more precise, you can here from the document type "NBIC" select the allowed item category blank. There in turn you can go one step further, to link purchase requisitions. There you can reference the PR document type "NB" and PR item category "U". Thereby a conversion can consider that PR items of type NB/U are convertible into PO items of type "NBIC/blank".

Kind regards

Thomas Jürgensen

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 PDF View doesn't work any more and have to do...
entry.content:
You need to set the parameter "isTrustedSource" to true.

In version 1.71.63 a new property "isTrustedSource" was introduced.:

[FIX] We've introduced the isTrustedSource property as a security enhancement for the PDFViewer control as detailed in the API reference

see API: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/1.71.70/#/api/sap.m.PDFViewer/controlProperties

Parameter to determine if the given PDF is from a trusted source. If the source is valid this property can be set to true. If isTrustedSource is set to true, the PDFViewer opens with the displayType set to "Embedded" on desktop devices. This means that the PDF content is directly shown within the PDFViewer. Set this property to true only when the PDF is generated by the application or the PDF is scanned for viruses. If isTrustedSource is set to false, the PDFViewer opens with the displayType set to "Link" on desktop devices. This means that any configuration that has been provided by the application for the property displayType is overridden. In this case, the user would need to download the PDF to view its content.

entry.title: Re: how to add an attribute of a different type in advanc...
entry.content:
Hello @Alex,
In this case you need to have Product Code as field in your Customer Review Model. 

Reason being - Advance Search work on the type of the attribute which is there in the main model. In your case customerReview is the main model, hence the  product attribute which is of type productModel can only be used.

In principle

  • For S-price items, the revaluation amount is posted to a price difference account.
  • For V-price items, the revaluation amount is recorded in a price difference account or in an inventory account, depending on the base quantity.

entry.title: Re: How can I see the previous (moved developments) devel...
entry.content:
By default there is no version management in target system, so you only see the last imported version in this system. 

  • To change behavior you have to change parameter VERS_AT_IMP in your system so import will now include a step 'Create versions before the import' (it won't be retroactive) - check @Sandra_Rossi 's answer
  • Else go to transaction SE03 to display the transport requests containing the object (and the main object if it's a sub-object: Function module > Function group, Program dynpro > Program, etc.)

But if you want to check (as you wrote) that live system version is the penultimate version of development, you already have the answer.

.

entry.title: Adobe Form -Schema based changes to be done (TEXT TRANSLATION)
entry.content:
Hi Community members,

The eco system is as follows.

logon language used TR.

Tcode used FTR03.

whenever the transaction is completed once adobe form is being called out 

i.e : FI_BILL_OF_EXCHANGE_LOC.

Current situaton.the entire page is getting printed in turkish.

Only one Particular text is getting printed in german.

please find attached screesnhot for the type of adobe form.

my understanding : IT is a standard adobe form which is being called.

so there is no context tab to make any changes.

this is the screenshot of the properties tab ie.1st image.

entry.title: Unable to use SAP API HUB to send REST APIs to SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I am trying to test REST APIs for SAP Digital Manufacturing using SAP API Hub (https://api.sap.com/package/SAPDigitalManufacturingCloud/overview). 

I followed this note (https://me.sap.com/notes/0003464193) to configure the environment, current config as following, clientID and clientSecret retrieved from the S4H Integration OAuth instance - service key on BTP, and copy pasted to SAP API HUB: 

The API i am testing is: https://api.sap.com/api/sapdme_inventory/tryout

When I click on 'Run', I have the error message: Invalid JWT. 

So I tried to test in Postman to ensue i have the valid token, however i retrieved the same error response: 

The Username and Password I used are the account I used to login to SAP DM, based on the error, seems like the end point is not expecting the token from my user, perhaps a different user (i.e., technical user?), if yes, where can i find this info?  

Any hints are appreciated, thanks in advance guys! 
SAP Digital Manufacturing API SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment

entry.title: Unlocking the Power of SAP: Step-by-Step Guide to Creating a Support Case
entry.content:
Encountering technical challenges or requiring expert support for your SAP system? Initiating a support case through SAP is a streamlined approach to ensure your inquiries receive prompt and professional attention from SAP's support team. In the video below, we provide a comprehensive, step-by-step walkthrough for creating a support case, enabling you to address issues effectively within the SAP platform.

Access Video here: https://learning.sap.com/videos/creating-a-support-case-in-sap-for-me 

To summarize the main steps in the video:

Step 1: Log in to SAP Support Portal

To create a support case, log in to SAP for Me using your S-user ID and password. If you don’t yet have an S-user ID, you can request one through your organization’s designated SAP contact or system administrator.

Please note that specific authorizations are required to create a support case in SAP for Me. Ensure that you have the necessary permissions assigned to your S-user ID to proceed with case creation.

  • Display Cases
  • Display All Cases
  • Report a Technical Issue
  • Sent Case to SAP
  • Closes Cases

For SuccessFactors & Other Cloud Solutions

  • Display Cloud Data

Step 2: Navigate to "Get Support Application”

Once you're logged in, navigate to the "Get Support Card" under Services & Support in SAP For Me. Here, you’ll access a form to enter detailed information about the issue you're encountering, allowing SAP support to better understand and address your concern.

Step 3: Provide Details about the Issue

In the support case form, you'll need to provide details about the issue you're experiencing. This includes a description of the problem, the affected system, and any relevant error messages or screenshots. The more information you can provide, the easier it will be for the SAP support team to assist you.

Step 4: Submit the Support Case

After completing the support case form, carefully review all entered information to ensure accuracy and completeness. Once confirmed, submit the case. You will receive an immediate confirmation of case creation, indicating that the request has been successfully logged. The case will then be routed to a support engineer, who will handle the assessment and resolution process. 

Step 5: Monitor the Status of the Case

You can monitor the progress of your support case by logging into SAP for Me and navigating to the "View Cases Card" section. In this area, you will find updates from the assigned support engineer regarding your case status. Additionally, you have the opportunity to provide any further information or clarification as needed, ensuring that the support team has all the relevant details to assist you effectively. 

By adhering to these outlined procedures, you can effectively ensure that your technical issues are addressed promptly and efficiently by the SAP support team.

Initiating a support case in SAP is the most effective method to obtain the necessary assistance, enabling your SAP system to operate seamlessly and maintain optimal performance.  

We hope this guide has been helpful in understanding the process of creating a support case in SAP. If you have any further questions or need additional assistance.

See Also

1296527 - How to create a support case for SAP Support - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

1339209 - Best practices for creating a support case - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have. 

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community 

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: Re: Customer exit SUSR0001 to log off invalid user
entry.content: Hi Sandra, With TH_POPUP, I see that if I add the statement if sy-subrc eq 0 then call FM SYST_LOGOFF, in this case, the popup displays for a quick second and instantly logs off the user. Basically, TH_POPUP does not wait for a user response and immediately calls the statement following it. So I changed it to POPUP_TO_INFORM where I can check if the sy-subrc eq 0, only then call the FM to log off the user. In this case, only if the user clicks the X mark or the green check mark in the popup, this user action will set the subrc as zero and then log off the user. In this case, user can see the popup, take his time to read the message and then click the green check mark which will log him off.

entry.title: Re: Customer exit SUSR0001 to log off invalid user
entry.content: I want the user to be able to read the popup message, understand that he is logging in outside the shift hours and then log him out. Otherwise, too many users will start calling the help desk complaining they are not able to login to SAP even though they enter the correct id and password.

entry.title: Re: Signavio - Usage
entry.content:
Hi Gupta, 

Thank you for your question! 

You can use different SAP Signavio products for the definition of As-Is and To-Be processes.

SAP Signavio Process Insights and SAP Signavio Process Intelligence can be used to identify how processes are executed in your organization. As-Is BPMN process models can be transferred from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence to SAP Signavio Process Manager. By doing so, you are able to enrich the BPMN diagrams with further information. SAP Signavio Process Insights allows you to assess the performance of specific process steps, highlighting how well they are executed and suggesting targeted improvements for better process quality. On this basis, you can model your To-Be processes in SAP Signavio Process Manager with data-driven improvement aspects.

If you have any further questions about the individual steps, please let me know!

Best regards

Jessica

entry.title: Re: Feature Request: Define custom colour palette in Sign...
entry.content:
Hello Anna, 

We have acknowledged the request and passed it on to the product management team. Thank you for the feedback! 


Best regards
Jessica
